2605.26158.title: Furina: Fragmented Uncertainty-Driven Refusal Instability Attack
2605.26158.paper_summary: Safety alignment in large language models (LLMs) and multimodal large language models (MLLMs) is commonly assumed to operate as a near-binary threshold mechanism. We challenge this assumption by revealing that safety behavior is governed by an instability region where small perturbations induce stochastic refusal decisions rather than deterministic outcomes. We develop a multi-metric diagnostic framework combining external and internal signals to characterize this instability. Through systematic experiments, we identify a characteristic diagnostic signature: inputs in unstable regimes exhibit elevated output uncertainty yet decreased internal safety activation, a decoupling phenomenon that explains why detection-based defenses fail against sophisticated attacks. Building on this framework, we introduce Furina, a jailbreak attack that deliberately induces this signature through fragmented, scene-anchored prompts without model-specific optimization. Furina outperforms strong single-turn and multi-turn baselines on HarmBench and achieves competitive results on MM-SafetyBench, demonstrating that uncertainty amplification provides a principled and transferable mechanism for understanding safety vulnerabilities. Code is available at: https://github.com/0xCavaliers/Furina_Jailbreak.
2605.26158.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）和多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）的安全性對齊通常被認為是一種近似二元閾值機制。<lb/>我們挑戰這一假設，揭示安全行為受一個不穩定區域的控制，在這裡小的擾動會引發隨機拒絕決策，而不是確定性結果。<lb/>我們開發了一個多指標診斷框架，結合外部和內部信號來描述這種不穩定性。<lb/>通過系統性實驗，我們識別出一個特徵診斷簽名：在不穩定區域中的輸入表現出較高的輸出不確定性，但內部安全激活卻降低，這是一種解耦現象，解釋了為什麼基於檢測的防禦在面對複雜攻擊時失敗。<lb/>基於這一框架，我們介紹了Furina，一種越獄攻擊，通過碎片化的、場景錨定的提示故意引發這一簽名，而不需要針對模型的特定優化。<lb/>Furina在HarmBench上超越了強大的單輪和多輪基準，並在MM-SafetyBench上取得了競爭性結果，證明不確定性放大提供了一種原則性和可轉移的機制來理解安全漏洞。<lb/>代碼可在以下網址獲得：https://github.com/0xCavaliers/Furina_Jailbreak。
2605.26158.author: Tongxi Wu et.al.
2605.26158.authors: Tongxi Wu, Jian Zhang, Yang Gao
